prompt: Create a finished editorial paper-collage still for a 9:16 image-to-video B-roll clip. Visual proposition: a stack of chat conversation papers being folded and locked inside a padlocked strongbox on the left, while on the right a separate service-provider viewing window shows only scrambled shredded-paper ciphertext texture — the real data stays encrypted, the provider sees only noise. Scene: perfectly flat dark-green paper field (#234A38) with subtle uncoated paper fiber. Style: premium editorial stop-motion paper collage; black-and-white halftone photographic cut-outs of conversation papers, a strongbox with a padlock, and a round porthole window filled with scrambled paper strips, with selective cream-white and brick-red padlock accents. Composition: vertical 9:16 locked poster frame; central subject within the middle 70 percent; generous clean color-field negative space; 3 to 6 large separable paper groups suitable for later assemble-from-empty animation. Materials: visible printed halftone dots, crisp machine-cut edges, thin warm-cream paper keylines, soft low-opacity physical drop shadows. Constraint: the relationship must read instantly — the true conversation is locked away, the provider's window shows only scrambled ciphertext. Avoid: no typography, no readable letters, no numerals, no logos, no watermark, no UI, no subtitles, no glossy 3D, no photoreal environment, no clutter.
